summary: DHH announces Strada, the final piece of the Hotwire triptych alongside Turbo and Stimulus. Strada bridges native mobile controls with web apps, allowing hybrid applications to feel native while being primarily web-driven. The release completes Hotwire's vision of enabling individual developers to build and deliver features across all platforms from a single codebase. DHH emphasizes three benefits: blurring the line between web and native, reducing app store dependency, and empowering web teams to ship mobile features independently.
key_points: Strada bridges native controls (menus, etc.) with Hotwire web apps, completing the Turbo/Stimulus/Strada triptych
Hybrid apps like HEY and Basecamp are nearly indistinguishable from fully native apps to most users, with immense productivity benefits
Reducing app store reliance lets teams deploy fixes instantly without waiting for bureaucratic approval
Web developers can now drive changes on native controls without coordinating with mobile teams
The Majestic Monolith approach lets a comparably tiny team serve multiple platforms from a single codebase
money_quotes: Turbo Native makes it easy to have native mobile apps driven by fast web views, Strada brings that extra bit of polish only native controls, like native menus, can provide.

While true native nerds can probably still tell the difference, the vast majority of paying customers cannot. And the productivity benefits to not writing everything as custom, native screens are immense.
One of the reasons that developing for the web feels so magical is because you can roll out changes and fixes to everyone instantly without asking or waiting for permission.
Every time you submit a new build, you have to factor in the risk that a bureaucrat will reject your application for reasons unknown.

The more power we can give the web developers to introduce new features on the Majestic Monolith that Just Works inside the mobile apps, the less we need to grind gears coordinating releases with the mobile team.
Supercharge the productivity of individual developers by making them capable of building and delivering complete features across all platforms by themselves.
No frontend/backend separation, and as little web/native separation as possible too.
key_insight: Strada completes Hotwire's vision of letting small web teams build fully native-feeling apps across every platform from a single codebase, maximizing developer productivity while minimizing platform-specific dependencies.
